properties.notes:
**Batch 1,2,3 - Scenario 30: Home Office Business Use**

Property used as main residence with one room (20% floor area) used for business from 2018. Home office expenses claimed.

**Timeline:**
- 1 Jul 2016 to 1 Jan 2018: Full main residence (1.5 years)
- 1 Jan 2018 to 1 Oct 2024: Main residence + home office (6.75 years)
  - 80% main residence
  - 20% business use (claimed home office expenses)
- Total ownership: 8.25 years

**Key Calculations:**
- Total capital gain: $559,825
- Business use portion: 20% × 6.75/8.25 years = 16.36% taxable
- Main residence portion: 83.64% exempt
- Taxable capital gain: $559,825 × 16.36% = $91,587
- After 50% CGT discount: $91,587 × 50% = $45,794
- **NET CAPITAL GAIN: $45,794**

**Expected Result:**
- NET CAPITAL GAIN = $45,794
- 16.36% of capital gain is taxable (business use proportion)

**ATO Rules Applied:**
- Section 118-190 ITAA 1997 (Business Use of Dwelling)
- When part of home used for business and expenses claimed, that portion not exempt
- Area × time apportionment method
- Section 115-25 ITAA 1997 (50% CGT discount)
